query: Elon Musk deposition DOGE staffers USAID dismantling case date court filing
a_issues: Content A lacks the key court-filing detail needed for the query. It has news summaries saying Musk was ordered deposed and later that Reuters reported he avoided deposition on March 4, 2026, but it does not include the underlying filing date/case citation or the specific court document text. An LLM could answer only partially and would have to infer the date from news coverage. It also mixes the district-court order and later appellate outcome without clarifying the filing sought in the query.
b_issues: Content B includes a secondary Yale JREG piece that appears to reference the case in prose rather than the actual filing, so it is less direct than a docket entry. The result set also includes some broader background items not specifically about the deposition order. But it does contain the Fourth Circuit PDF with the filed date and description of the protective-order dispute, plus a case clearinghouse entry with the case number and chronology.
reasoning: Content B better enables a correct answer because it contains the most important anchor fact from an actual court filing: the Fourth Circuit document showing 'FILED: March 4, 2026' in case No. 26-1160 / 8:25-cv-00462-TDC, and describing that the issue was the order denying a protective order against depositions of Musk, Marocco, and Lewin. That directly supports answering a question asking for the date and court filing in the Elon Musk/DOGE/USAID dismantling deposition matter. Content A mostly offers media reports (Axios, BI, Reuters, Independent) and does not surface the underlying court filing itself or its filing date as clearly.
